titleStmt.title: SII 38.450: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 450. (A.R. No. 450 of 1912.) KŌṬṬŪR, MANNARGUDI TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT. On the south wall, maṇḍapa infront of the central shrine Koḻundīśvara temple.

p.hi: King: Tribhuvanachakaravarttigaḷ Rājarāja III Year 8+1: 1225 A.D.

summary.p: Records that Nāgamaṅgalam-uḍaiyāṉ Tiruambalampiriyādān ambalam-kōyil koṇḍāṉ consecrated an image of Tiruñānampeṟṟa-piḷḷaiyār (saint Jñānasambandar) at Koḻundāṇdār temple at Koṭṭūr in Neṉmali-nāḍu (a sub-division) of Rājēndrachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. It is also stated that the trustees of the temple who received two thousand kāśu from the same person for providing food offerings to the deity (set up by him) agreed to supply two nāḻi of rice per day.

Further, it is also recorded that Sūrasāraṁbi Kumāravishṇu Bhaṭṭaṉ, his younger brother Tiruttuṟaināyaga Bhaṭṭaṉ and (a lady) Alliyaṅ Kōdai Sāṉi, the wife of Kumāravishṇu Bhaṭṭaṉ, who is described as her guardian, donated land as tirunāmattukkāṇi to the same deity.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrīḥ tribhuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīirājarājatevarkku yāṇṭu Eṭṭāvatiṉ Etirāmāṇṭu rā
jentracoḻavaḷanāṭṭu neṉmalināṭṭuk koṭṭuruṭaiyār koḻuntāṇṭārkoyilukku nākamaṅkalamu
ṭaiyāṉ tiruampalampiriyātāṉ Ampalaṅkoyil koṇṭāṉ Eḻuntaruḷuvitta tiruñānampeṟṟa pi
ḷḷaiyārku Ādiśaṇḍeśvaratevar tiruaruḷāl Ippiḷḷaiyārku tiruamutu Aricikku Ikkoyil
tānattom Ivar pakkal UṭaiyamākaRead upaiyamākakkaikkoṇṭa kācu 2 1000 Ikkācu Iraṇṭāyirattukkum nāḷ
Oṉṟukku Irunāḻi Arici Ākappalakaippuṟatte cantirātittavarai Aḷakkak kaṭavomāka sam
madittom Ivvaṉaivom � § Ippiḷḷaiyārku surasārambi kumāraviṣṇapaṭṭanum Ivaṉ tampi tiruttuṟaināya
ka paṭṭanum Ikkumāraviṣṇapaṭṭaṉai mutukaṇṇāka Uṭaiya Ivaṉ bhāri Alliyaṅkotaic cāniyu
m tirunāmattukkāṇiāka dharmmatānamākak kuṭutta śrī kulottuṅka coḻaccaturveti maṅkala
rāja ṉan ḥ ṭi nilam Araikkāṇi muntirikaik kiḷ
munṟumākkāṇi Araikkāṇi ḥ—
Digital edition of SII 38.450 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
